item.title: engine.ts: VM left running and SSH key orphaned if registry write throws after successful VM creation
item.intent: A provisioning failure after VM creation must never leave the user with an untracked running VM. If the instance registry write fails, the user must receive the VM ID so they can clean it up manually — silent orphans are a security and cost risk.
item.description: In src/cloud/provisioning/engine.ts around line 234, addInstance() is called without a try-catch after the VM has been created and the firewall applied. If writeInstanceRegistry() throws (disk full, permissions error), the error propagates uncaught and the provisioning function rejects. The VM is running, the firewall is in place, and the SSH private key is on disk at keypair.privateKeyPath — but no registry entry exists. clawhq deploy list will never show the instance. The user has an untracked running VM with no way to destroy it via clawhq. Fix: wrap addInstance() in try-catch; on failure, include the provider instance ID in the error message so the user can destroy it manually via the cloud console.
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/engine.ts lines 231-260 to understand the addInstance() call and surrounding flow.

Wrap the addInstance() call (line 234) in a try-catch. On catch, return a failure result that includes: the provider instance ID, the SSH key path, and instructions to destroy the VM manually via the cloud console. Do NOT clean up the SSH key or attempt to destroy the VM inside this error handler — those are separate concerns.
Write a test in engine.test.ts: mock writeFileSync (via registry) to throw on the registry write, verify the returned result has success:false and the providerInstanceId in the error message.
Run npm test -- --testPathPattern=engine.test to confirm all tests pass.
acceptanceCriteria: If addInstance() throws after VM creation, provisionInstance() returns success:false with the provider instance ID and a message directing the user to destroy it manually
The SSH key path is included in the error output so the user can locate the orphaned key
Existing engine tests pass with no regression
